DATE: The first album by Gorillaz following the Tomorrow Comes Today EP. The album was made in collaboration with Deltron 3030 members Dan the Automator (also of Handsome Boy Modelling School, Lovage, etc.) and Kid Koala, with Del the Funky Homosapien featuring on "Clint Eastwood" and "Rock the House".

Name:
Demon Days
(prod. Gorillaz & Danger Mouse)
(Celebrity Harvest, We Are Happy Landfill)
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord):
(March 26, 2001) & (December 12, 2001) (Gorillaz & G-Sides are officially released)
(May 11, 2005) & (November 19, 2007) (Demon Days & D-Sides are officially released)
DATE: With the goal of proving Gorillaz wasn’t a gimmick, Demon Days featured a noticeable shift in sound from the dub-influenced underground hip-hop of Gorillaz to a more political rock, pop, choral and hip-hop route brought on by string arrangements, drum-sequencing, and lyrics crticizing the Afghanistan War and life post-911. Co-produced by then-newcomer Danger Mouse, Demon Days is also the first Gorillaz album to heavily feature other artists and rely on collaboration, a future mainstay for all Gorillaz projects.

Name: Plastic Beach
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord):
(January 14, 2009) (Damon plays three demos on BBC Radio 1)
(March 3, 2010) (Plastic Beach officially releases)
DATE: Conceived initially as three separate projects: Carousel, Plastic Beach and Dee, The Opera. The third Gorillaz album took on a more environmental focus from Damon observing the amounts of plastic on the sands of a beach. Plastic Beach is infamous for going over the band's budget, and its scrapped content, leading to the 2018 #FreeTheSea campaign calling for the release of the 200+ unreleased songs recorded during the making of Phase 3.

Name: The Fall
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord):
(December 25, 2010) & (April 19, 2011) (The Fall officially releases)
(April 2012) (Gorillaz enter three-year hiatus)
DATE: "The Fall" is the fourth album by Gorillaz, infamous for being concieved on tour, on an iPad. It was first released as a download for members of the Gorillaz fan club on December 25th, 2010, followed by a wider physical release on April 19th, 2011 by Parlophone Records.

Name: iELECTRIBE Gorillaz Edition Stems
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord): Files from the app, includes some samples, some stems and brief alt takes of songs from The Fall.

Name:
HUMANZ
(prod. Gorillaz, The Twilite Tone & Remi Kabaka Jr.)
(Transformerz)
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord):
(January 30, 2015) (Jamie Hewlett posts new artwork of Murdoc & Noodle on Instagram)
(April 27, 2017) (Humanz officially releases)
DATE: After reuniting at a party, Damon and Jamie would begin work on a Gorillaz comeback album in 2015 titled Transformerz. After producer The Twilite Tone became involved in 2016, Transformerz would be renamed to Humanz, with a new concept imagining a party at the end of the world caused by (the then-hypothetical of) Donald Trump winning the 2016 US presidential election.

The Now Now
(prod. Gorillaz, James Ford & Remi Kabaka Jr.)
(3-D)
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord):
(April 27, 2017) (Humanz officially releases)
(June 29, 2018) (The Now Now officially releases)
DATE: Recorded during the Humanz Tour with the intention of having more Damon-centric songs to play at concerts after the reaction to his absence on the majority of Humanz. The Now Now is a largely funk-inspired album written from the perspective of 2-D, with the change in sound being accredited in-universe to the temporary substitution of Murdoc to Ace from The Powerpuff Girls as the band's bassist.

Name: Humility [Instrumental]                                                         (Humility SSMix5 InstMastered1644)
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord): Instrumental to Humilty higher quality file leaked during the 2024 Warner leak

Name:
Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez
(Almanac CD)
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord):
(June 29, 2018) (The Now Now officially releases)
(October 23, 2020) (Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez officially releases)
DATE: Setting out to not make an album, Damon, Jamie, and longtime collaborator and newly credited Gorillaz member Remi Kabaka Jr began work on releasing singles accompanied by music videos near-monthly throughout 2020 in a series titled Song Machine. However, after the rise of the COVID-19 pandemic in March, more songs would be created through Zoom and emails, leading to the first season of Song Machine being expanded into an album released as Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez.

Name: Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez [Instrumentals]
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord): Released across 7" records in the Super Deluxe box set. Only includes the main album's instrumentals.

Name:
Cracker Island
(prod. Gorillaz & Greg Kurstin)
(Song Machine, Season Two)
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord):
(August 26, 2021) (Meanwhile officially releases)
(February 24, 2023) (Cracker Island officially releases)
DATE: Originally announced as the second season of Song Machine in October 2020; Jamie, through the Gorillaz Unofficial 2020 Plastic Beach Fanzine, would reveal that the project following Song Machine would take on a narrative-heavy structure, describing it as a "more in-depth kinda epic story with the characters and music. Not just an album, but bigger." Cracker Island would topically revolve around cults and the Los Angeles lifestyle, where the album was recorded in 9-11 days with album collaborator Greg Kurstin.

Name:
The Mountain
(prod. Remi Kabaka Jr, James Ford & ???)
Notes (Gorillaz Leaks Discord):
(February 24, 2023) (Cracker Island officially releases)
(September 3, 2025) (Mystery Show previews several album tracks)
(Ongoing)
DATE: Vaguely hinted at in their 2023 Coachella interview, the ninth Gorillaz album will be recorded somewhere they'd "never been before", likely Mumbai as teased in recent social media posts. Several tracks from the album were played at a mystery show on September 3rd, 2025. It is very likely this new album is in some way a spiritual successor to Plastic Beach, having several unused tracks from the era repurposed.
